choice: Independent normal draws with per-Scenario mean/SD and no return cap
rationale: Two binding priors jointly settle this. First, 9032d3c9 explicitly states the v1 engine improves on Excel mechanics out of the gate and treats Excel simplifications as defects to fix — the +20% return cap is exactly such a simplification, so it is dropped. Second, 2949f831 defines a Scenario as a named assumption set that includes 'return/inflation distributions' as per-Scenario inputs, establishing that distribution parameters (mean/SD) are configurable per Scenario rather than hard-coded. Together these mandate: normal sampling (already used by the Excel, now without the arbitrary cap) driven by per-Scenario mean/SD parameters. Historical bootstrap is not implied by any binding prior and the advisory context explicitly defers it to post-v1.
